raw_consumer_statement:
This complaint is for the disputes : XXXX, XXXX, XXXX, XXXX, XXXX, XXXX, XXXX. 

On XX/XX/XXXX, I booked flight for my family on XXXX for a total of {$21.00}. However, it appears that they charged me 7 times of {$890.00}. This is an overcharge which is already investigated by XXXX XXXX and as well as XXXX. I contacted them by phone on XX/XX/XXXX, XX/XX/XXXX, XX/XX/XXXX. They got back to me on XX/XX/XXXX promised me a refund by email by sending me a credit slip. XXXX XXXX also emailed me back on XX/XX/XXXX confirming the same. Please see the attached email of confirmation for the evidence. Please help me with this dispute and get my money back. 

I contacted AMEX on XX/XX/XXXX to file disputes for those charges. As those charges are at the same amount, AMEX customer service and dispute team agent had a hard time to file them correct. As of today, after many re-open and explanation to AMEX, the people are still messing up the dispute reason for these disputes and this is the main reason why I can not get my money back. AMEX agents open duplicate, wrong dispute that made there are more than 40 disputes opened for 7 charges. I understand this might be confusing but this could be handled in a much more professional way. See attached file for the confusion. 

I would like to dispute this as XXXX ( the travel agency ) wrongly charged my account and XXXX XXXX ( The airline ) already confirmed that. They all promised me a refund for all 7 charges more than 4 weeks ago and till now I have not received anything. See attached file for the evidence from Airlines and Agency.
